refnamediv.refpurpose: Returns a simplified representation of a geometry, using the Visvalingam-Whyatt algorithm

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Returns a simplified representation of a geometry using the Visvalingam-Whyatt algorithm. The simplification tolerance is an area value, in the units of the input SRS. Simplification removes vertices which form "corners" with area less than the tolerance. The result may not be valid even if the input is.
The function can be called with any kind of geometry (including GeometryCollections), but only line and polygon elements are simplified. Endpoints of linear geometry are preserved.

note.para: The returned geometry may lose its simplicity (see ), topology may not be preserved, and polygonal results may be invalid (see ). Use to preserve topology and ensure validity. also preserves topology and validity.
This function does not preserve boundaries shared between polygons. Use if this is required.
This function handles 3D and the third dimension will affect the result.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: A LineString is simplified with a minimum-area tolerance of 30.
refsection.programlisting:
SELECT ST_AsText(ST_SimplifyVW(geom,30)) simplified FROM (SELECT 'LINESTRING(5 2, 3 8, 6 20, 7 25, 10 10)'::geometry AS geom) AS t;
simplified ------------------------------ LINESTRING(5 2,7 25,10 10)
